notes: v405 adds a read-only combat VFX placement preview helper for future editor inspection.
v405 summarizes player class/gender attack VFX placements and tutorial monster attack VFX placements in one preview contract.
v405 adds a quick-check guard so VFX placement preview metadata stays complete and service-worker cached.
v404 adds class-aware player attack VFX placement profiles for warrior, assassin, fighter, archer, wand-book mage, and staff mage sprites.
v404 tunes player attack VFX by effect type so pierce, projectile, magic, dark, explosion, slash, and impact hits read differently.
v404 extends the player battle sprite guard so attack VFX placement metadata stays connected to combat rendering.
v403 adds monster-specific attack VFX placement profiles for the tutorial island enemy roster.
v403 lets enemy attack effects carry offset and slash-size variables so small, beast, construct, heavy, knight, and boss attacks read differently.
v403 extends the monster display guard so VFX placement metadata and CSS variables remain connected.
v402 replaces the Project Regressor representative icon with the newly provided blue crystal emblem.
v402 syncs the web header, PWA install icon, manifest icon, and Android launcher icons to the same source image.
v402 bumps the cache and update channel so installed apps receive the refreshed icon package.
v401 routes enemy attack VFX through monster SFX profiles instead of using one generic impact effect.
v401 keeps hyper enemy attacks dark while normal monster attacks can render slash, pierce, impact, explosion, or dark feedback.
v401 extends the monster display guard so SFX profile mappings and runtime motion SFX ids stay connected.
v400 adds monster battle sprite runtime placement presets for every tutorial island monster.
v400 exposes enemy motion, SFX profile, and foot-baseline safe-margin metadata to the combat card runtime.
v400 adds a separate target hit flash while keeping the combat card frame and resource bars fixed.
v399 applies the battle sprite motion runtime pack with transform-only player and enemy sprite motions.
v399 keeps combat cards, bars, damage text, and slash SFX on separate layers while the sprite image moves independently.
v399 adds a quick-check guard for sprite motion ids, runtime triggers, service-worker caching, and no combat-card hit shake.
v398 adds a tutorial key-event dialogue resolver for the v2.6.3 final package event details.
v398 lets future system-window or codex-record surfaces retrieve localized event-detail copy by tutorial event id.
v398 extends the tutorial dialogue check so resolved event details fill dynamic placeholders without leaking braces.

v397 promotes the tutorial dialogue catalog to v2.6.3_FINAL with dynamic placeholders and pre-reality forbidden term keys.
v397 guards the shore status recheck and drifting-slime branch so status grade stays dynamic and weapon copy remains generic.
v396 separates non-equipment loot acquisition logs from equipment candidate insight.
v396 routes codex-card, mana-crystal, and skill-fragment drops through a dedicated 전리품 획득 log format and system-window notice.
v396 extends loot, combat-log, and system-window checks so non-equipment loot cannot be mistaken for 추천 장착 equipment.
v395 applies ProjectRegressor_v2.6.2_FIX with dynamic initial stat summaries and status-grade placeholders.
v395 opens first-run stat totals to a guarded 10-13 range and derives F-/F/F+ status grades from the actual total.
v395 adds a dynamic status-grade quick-check so fixed STR 2 and hard-coded 상태창 등급 F tutorial copy cannot return.
v394 adds the first non-equipment tutorial loot catalog for codex-card fragments, mana-crystal fragments, and skill fragments.
v394 connects those loot items to early monster drop tables and shows them in drop previews and inventory without treating them as equipment.
v394 adds a loot item catalog quick-check so monster drop ids must resolve through the combined item catalog.
v393 applies ProjectRegressor_v2.6.1_FIX so pre-tutorial screens no longer imply a Hunter Association or guild society already exists.
v393 changes early registration, abyss question, completion, status-tooltip, and rusty starter weapon copy to system/test-neutral wording.
v393 adds a pre-tutorial hunter-association boundary quick-check so forbidden early terms cannot return.
v392 applies the first ProjectRegressor_v2.6_FINAL package pass as a guarded final contract.
v392 upgrades the tutorial dialogue catalog to v2.6_FINAL with new-game flow, key flags, starter-skill aliases, and 5-plus loop dialogue metadata.
v392 adds a final-package quick-check for initial stats, basic attack start, starter card skill aliases, damage-threshold boss evaluation, and loop dialogue cycling.
v391 adds the first v2.5 expanded-dialogue pass for disposition and starter-card reactions.

v389 aligns the starting objective with the v2.4.1 four-step tutorial guidance.

v387 keeps the v2.4.1 opening guidance visible without adding a blocking dialogue screen.

v386 aligns level-up help and log text with the v2.4.1 +2 free-stat balance.
